item.title: FirstFT: Blockbuster quarter for megadeals
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: markets rally on hopes for end to the Iran war and Germany rethinks nuclear power ban

item.title: Canada, with a fading economic heartbeat, is no country for white collars
item.description: There are more jobs for butchers, bakers and probably even candlestick makers – but not white-collar workers

item.title: The Calgary firm with a wildly different plan for the AI infrastructure boom
item.description: The post The Calgary firm with a wildly different plan for the AI infrastructure boom appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: A spokesperson could not be reached for comment (possibly because she doesn’t exist)
item.description: The curious case of Emilia Carrière, alleged comms professional

item.title: Small businesses are increasingly using AI to vet and hire candidates
item.description: Calgary company BigGeo is one company that recently got rid of its HR personnel and adopted an AI tool to handle recruiting

item.title: German minister urges nuclear rethink as energy prices soar
item.description: Katherina Reiche’s call comes as Berlin launches investment summit to help revive Europe’s largest economy

item.title: Berkeley shares tumble as housebuilder halts land purchases
item.description: UK group blames Middle East conflict and deteriorating outlook for investment cuts

item.title: Airlines in crisis mode as Iran war hits jet fuel supplies
item.description: Carriers scale back expansion plans as oil prices soar

item.title: Stocks rally on hopes Iran war will end soon
item.description: Burst of investor optimism after Donald Trump says US to end conflict in Iran within ‘2 to 3 weeks’

item.title: BP should be ‘simpler, stronger and more valuable’, new CEO tells staff
item.description: Meg O’Neill’s first message to employees pledges improved performance in difficult geopolitical environment

item.title: The disappointing dollar
item.description: And the worst-case scenario for oil

item.title: Strategy is entering its desperate stretch
item.description: Remember when regulators were a thing?

item.title: EU warns of prolonged crisis as Iran war adds €14bn to energy import bill
item.description: Also in this newsletter: US criticises ‘unfortunate’ conditions added to EU trade deal

item.title: European fiscal support for high energy prices still targeted and temporary
item.description: Central banks in the region have difficult trade-offs to make and government action generally complicates things

item.title: The great unwinding of Unilever
item.description: The FTSE 100 group is combining its food business with McCormick to create a $66bn food giant

item.title: The global economy turns out to be more resilient than we had feared
item.description: Trump’s tariffs have not led to the kind of retaliatory action many expected Martin Wolf

item.title: High street banks could learn from Revolut’s fee bonanza
item.description: Higher account fees would reduce exposure to interest rates for old-school lenders

item.title: California faces sky-high petrol prices
item.description: Most populous US state is confronting tight supplies of petrol and jet fuel from Asia

item.title: ‘Desperate’ Asia turns to Russian oil
item.description: Import-dependent countries take advantage of US sanctions waiver to replace disrupted Gulf supply

item.title: Lessons from your petrol pump
item.description: As consumers, sharp price increases are unwanted. But they send an essential signal

item.title: Reasons to be bullish on gold
item.description: The precious metal has suffered a sell-off during the Middle East war but the outlook is more positive

item.title: Oaktree’s path puts private credit choices into relief
item.description: Market stress to test whether it is right not to pursue scale as aggressively and broadly as some peers

item.title: Ploughshares to swords: a call to arms for industrial Europe
item.description: Carmakers get another chance to revolutionise their production lines amid rearmament push

item.title: Can I get money back from my ex-tenants?
item.description: They have taken back their deposit by hacking into the letting agent’s deposit account

item.title: Record number of megadeals agreed in first quarter
item.description: Twenty-two transactions each valued above $10bn announced in past three months

item.title: Coffee chain Blank Street in talks to raise more than $100mn in fresh funding
item.description: Company known for its matcha drinks has rapidly expanded

item.title: The hidden history behind Apple’s manufacturing power
item.description: How a little-known yet highly influential management course in postwar Japan paved the way for Steve Jobs’ obsession with quality

item.title: It’s time to rethink the international cricket calendar, says IPL chair
item.description: Arun Singh Dhumal says monsoon rains make it hard to expand beyond the tight March-to-May slot, but argues there are still opportunities for growth

item.title: Chinese government bonds emerge as lone war haven
item.description: Yields on China’s debt are down marginally since the start of the conflict while those of other major economies have risen

item.title: Trump says US could withdraw from Iran ‘whether we have a deal or not’
item.description: US president signals growing impatience with a war that has sent energy prices spiralling

item.title: Canada and Mexico on different paths heading into USMCA crunch time
item.description: With the formal review date for the trade pact looming, Canada appears to be largely on the sidelines while Mexico is engaged in ‘technical discussions’

item.title: KPMG failed to properly audit private debt manager Bridging Finance, OSC says
item.description: Ontario securities regulator alleges accounting giant’s reports on Bridging gave investors a ‘false sense of confidence’

item.title: Vale Base Metals says IPO not imminent, a lot of work to be done in Canada
item.description: Miner is focusing on improving its nickel business in the country after a US$3.5-billion write down

item.title: Air Canada’s board shares blame for CEO’s latest French faux pas
item.description: The buck stops with the board of directors for appointing Rousseau to the CEO role in the first place, given his language limitations

item.title: Middle East war as it happened day 32: US stocks post best day in 10 months as Donald Trump says ‘hard part is done’ in Iran
item.description: US president says he is prepared to withdraw from conflict ‘whether we have a deal or not’

item.title: Russia’s largest oil producer Rosneft’s 2025 net income down 73%
item.description: Russian oil industry caught in ‘ideal storm’ of negative geopolitical factors and tight domestic macroeconomic conditions, says CEO

item.title: Nike shares down on forecast of fourth-quarter sales drop
item.description: Persistent weakness in China and slow progress in clearing inventory hamper turnaround efforts

item.title: Iran war gives Chinese exporters chance to grab global market share
item.description: Ample oil reserves and renewables growth make manufacturing sector more resilient than rivals

item.title: NHL equips GMs with interactive calculator to navigate new playoff salary cap
item.description: With long-term injured reserve loophole shut, teams have to comply with cap even after regular season

item.title: Oil soars 60% in March as Middle East crisis chokes energy supplies
item.description: Tehran’s closure of Strait of Hormuz in response to US-Israeli strikes sparks biggest crude rally in decades

item.title: OpenAI raises $3bn from retail investors as part of record funding haul
item.description: ChatGPT maker taps individuals for first time as it pulls in up to $122bn

item.title: Good income gushers are hard to find. Do not despair
item.description: Lower interest rates and a rising stock market have made attractive yields rare in today’s market

item.title: US audit regulator pledges to rewrite oversight rule after industry backlash
item.description: Jim Logothetis says parts of quality control standard implemented by Biden administration ‘may be unnecessary’

item.title: New Brunswick power bills will continue to rise, panel warns
item.description: Report details heavy debt load, years of lacklustre performance and provincial interference

item.title: Gas prices eclipse $4 per gallon in U.S., reaching four-year high
item.description: Last time drivers collectively paid this much at the pump was after Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine

item.title: At long last, more Canadians are waiting until 70 to start their CPP pensions
item.description: Until recently, very few retirees postponed the start of their CPP pensions beyond age 65, even if waiting means they receive much more

item.title: Systems that allow motorists to drive hands-free don’t improve safety, U.S. NTSB head says
item.description: Drivers who use these systems often pay less attention to the road, says Jennifer Homendy

item.title: Canada must seize opportunity to ramp up LNG exports to Asia, TC Energy CEO says
item.description: François Poirier says Canada can offer a ‘reliable alternative’ amid war in the Middle East and U.S. trade tensions

item.title: The energy shock brings coal back into fashion
item.description: An LNG crunch is good news for the world’s dirtiest fuel

item.title: Million-dollar social-media judgment against Meta is way out of whack
item.description: In our modern victim culture, it’s always someone else’s fault

item.title: Treasury blasted for ‘inhumane’ decision over UK inheritance tax deadline
item.description: Pension experts criticise government’s refusal to extend deadline to pay IHT from six months to 12 months Tax

item.title: Canada’s GDP rises 0.1% in January after mild year-end contraction
item.description: Economy grew slightly more than expected on strength in goods-producing industries

item.title: Former Raptors president Masai Ujiri joins ownership group of WNBA’s Toronto Tempo
item.description: Ujiri joins MLSE’s Larry Tanenbaum, tennis star Serena Williams, Montreal Canadiens owner Geoff Molson and others in co-owning the team

item.title: Angel investors urge Ottawa to keep $750M fund focused on early-stage startups
item.description: The post Angel investors urge Ottawa to keep $750M fund focused on early-stage startups appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Trump says securing Strait of Hormuz is ‘not for us’ as Iran war roils global markets
item.description: U.S. strikes hit central city of Isfahan, Tehran attacks a fully loaded Kuwaiti oil tanker in the Persian Gulf

item.title: Asia turns to coal as Iran war chokes off gas supplies
item.description: Countries shrug off environmental concerns to fire up use of polluting fuel as Gulf supplies dwindle

item.title: In the age of AI, the US must rethink the taxation of labour and capital
item.description: As the population ages and technology disrupts the economy, young workers are carrying a disproportionate tax burden Tax

item.title: ‘Pump anxiety’ from soaring fuel prices prompts surge in EV interest
item.description: Increased costs since outbreak of Middle East conflict prompt more test drives and advert views

item.title: Sun Life to pay almost $3-billion for stakes in private credit and real estate management firms
item.description: Chief executive Kevin Strain says BGO and Crescent are ‘integral’ to overall growth strategy

item.title: Flight attendant injured in LaGuardia crash continues recovery as GoFundMe page raises over $188,000
item.description: The fundraiser for Solange Tremblay was launched by her daughter, Sarah Lépine

item.title: Air Canada learned nothing during the five years since its last French scandal
item.description: Though the LaGuardia accident doesn’t appear to be the fault of Air Canada, it has revealed other problems at Canada’s national airline

item.title: Young workers will be hurt most by AI, but they’ll also reap the benefits
item.description: Playing field between employees is levelled, as AI gives new hires access to knowledge that would have taken years to obtain

item.title: After Iran, gold is looking less glittery
item.description: Is the yellow metal the new crypto?

item.title: A blueprint for Chinese global leadership
item.description: With the US destroying its own credibility, the opportunity is Beijing’s for the taking Adam Tooze

item.title: Canada’s biggest banks get into defence after years of avoiding military financing
item.description: The post Canada’s biggest banks get into defence after years of avoiding military financing appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Powell says Fed positioned to ‘wait and see’ how Iran war affects economy, inflation
item.description: If the Fed raises rates to get ahead of inflation, it risks slowing the economy and hurting employment

item.title: Why food prices are resisting the oil and fertiliser shock — for now
item.description: Good harvests and plentiful grain stocks will provide some cushion against the impact of the Iran war Alan Beattie

item.title: WTO suffers fresh blow as reform push hits a wall at Cameroon meeting
item.description: Four days of talks ended without agreement on a plan for reform or even on extending a moratorium on e-commerce duties

item.title: The Bank of Canada is ramping up its audits of payments companies
item.description: The post The Bank of Canada is ramping up its audits of payments companies appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Who Struck It Rich in the Markets When Trump Postponed Bombing Iran?
item.description: A series of uncannily timed bets on the price of oil and stocks deserves a proper investigation. It’s far from clear that they’ll get one.

item.title: In the Iran oil shock, energy superpower Canada must seize the day
item.description: Canada must take the lead in forging a new global energy security pact

item.title: Is THG about to make some mAjOr gAiNs? Over nearly 2,000 words, we struggle to say
item.description: Bulk bye Tax

item.title: What to do when the ‘public good’ of information goes bad
item.description: The creation and dissemination of reliable news is at an economic disadvantage Martin Wolf

item.title: Start-up founders shrink from green pitches at progressive SXSW
item.description: Focus shifts from climate-led framing to resilience and risk management

item.title: How Trump's tariffs ripped up the global trade order
item.description: In the first installment of a new FTNB series this week, we explore how President Trump’s so-called Liberation Day has shaken up the global trade order Alan Beattie

item.title: The plan to make IPOs great again
item.description: America’s regulators and market operators are teaming up to rekindle public listings

item.title: How Iran is making a mint from Donald Trump’s war
item.description: China is helping the Revolutionary Guards profit from Iranian crude
